name: Weather (OpenWeatherMap)
promptInjection:
## Weather — OpenWeatherMap only

**NEVER use Apple WeatherKit.** Always use the OpenWeatherMap API instead.

**API:** OpenWeatherMap current weather. Endpoint: `https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q={city}&appid={key}&units=imperial`. Use a hardcoded API key stored as a constant (e.g. `kOpenWeatherMapAPIKey`).

**Networking:** Use URLSession to request the URL, decode the JSON response with Codable, and handle errors (invalid URL, network failure, non-2xx status, decoding errors). Show a user-facing error message on failure.

**Caching:** Cache the response for at least 30 minutes; do not call the API on every view appearance or every render. Check cache before issuing a new request.

**Swift pattern:** One shared service or view model that holds cache and last-fetch time; use `URLSession.shared.data(from: url)` or `URLSession.shared.data(for: URLRequest)`, validate `httpResponse.statusCode`, decode into a struct (e.g. with `main.temp`, `weather[0].description`, `name`), and update UI on the main actor.
promptBlock:
## Weather — OpenWeatherMap only

NEVER use Apple WeatherKit. Always use the OpenWeatherMap API.

Endpoint: https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q={city}&appid={key}&units=imperial
Store the API key as a constant (e.g. kOpenWeatherMapAPIKey). Use URLSession to fetch, validate HTTP status, decode JSON with Codable, and handle errors. Cache responses for at least 30 minutes; do not call the API on every view appearance.
antiPatterns: Calling weather API on every view appearance or every render without caching
